title: Zerion Alternative for Syndicates, Funds and Angel Groups: When Tracking Alone Is Not Enough
content_html:
What is a good Zerion alternative, and when do you actually need one? If you are an individual investor tracking your own wallets, Zerion is a genuinely strong choice and you may not need an alternative at all. The moment you invest as a group, however, the requirements change. A syndicate, angel group, fund or launchpad needs member management, structured raises with vesting and distributions, and a way to trade allocations. That is investor operations, not just tracking, and it is where Cryptool positions itself as the Zerion alternative for groups. Cryptool keeps the non-custodial, read-only tracking foundation and adds the operational layer on top.

What Zerion does well

Credit where it is due. Zerion is a popular self-custodial wallet and portfolio app. It aggregates tokens, NFTs and DeFi positions across many networks, shows live prices and profit and loss, and lets users keep control of their own keys. For a solo investor who wants one clean view of on-chain activity, that is a well-executed product, and nothing in this post argues otherwise.

Where group investing needs more

Wallet-level tracking answers "what do I hold and what is it worth". Group investing raises different questions. Who are my members and what tier of access does each one get? How do I run a round with caps, a whitelist and KYC? How do vesting schedules and claim windows get enforced, and who reminds investors when unlocks are due? What happens when a member wants to exit an allocation before it fully vests? A wallet app is not designed to answer these, because it was never meant to. They belong to a different category of tool.

What Cryptool covers

Cryptool is an investor management platform for web3 investors, angels, syndicates, funds and launchpads. It is non-custodial by design. Wallet connections are read-only by default, and Cryptool never holds private keys, funds or signing authority.

Portfolio. Individual or group investments in one view, including tokens, unvested allocations and custom assets, with P&L and ROI indicators and staking unlock schedules. Coverage spans all chains and all wallets for over 90% of platform features, and on-chain modules are live on Ethereum, BNB Chain and MultiversX, with more coming.

Groups. Group and member management with up to 5 custom member tiers, pool creation and management, and ROI and P&L per group. This is the part a personal wallet app has no equivalent for.

Raise. Multi-stage rounds with configurable caps, vesting schedules and claim windows, plus whitelist and KYC. Once a raise completes, distribution happens automatically on-chain. Investors get reminders when unlocks are due, and allocations update at least hourly.

Market. Allocation trading, partial or whole, including unvested or partially unvested positions, alongside public trading, broker sales and OTC execution between qualified counterparties. Full terms are published at Market and OTC terms.

Calendar. Real-time notifications for vesting updates and project events, so nobody in the group misses a claim window.

Choosing between them

This is not really a head-to-head. Zerion serves the individual investor tracking and using their own wallets. Cryptool serves the operator running money with other people involved. Many groups will find their members happily use a wallet app day to day while the syndicate itself runs raises, member tiers and allocation trades on Cryptool. Plans start at Free ($0), with For Users from $2.99/mo, For Advanced Users from $7.99/mo, Manager for VC and funds from $39.99/mo, and Enterprise from $119.99/mo. Details are at services and pricing.

Common questions

Is Cryptool custodial? No. Wallet connections are read-only by default, and Cryptool never holds private keys, funds or signing authority. You track and operate; custody stays with you and your members.

Can I manage a syndicate or angel group on Cryptool? Yes. You can manage groups and members with up to 5 custom member tiers, create and manage pools, and see ROI and P&L per group in one view.

Can members trade allocations before they fully vest? Yes. The Market module supports allocation trading, partial or whole, including unvested or partially unvested allocations, plus broker sales and OTC execution between qualified counterparties.

How do I get started or ask a question? Start on the Free plan, review services and pricing, or reach the team through the contact page.
